response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and open-source credentials, but their background is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture work, hardware-software co-design, or deep-learning workload optimization. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments, which falls well short of the hardware performance architecture expertise required at principal level.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not hardware performance architecture
